feat: full macOS support with three container runtimes (#8)

* feat: full macOS support with three container runtimes

Add comprehensive macOS support (Apple Silicon and Intel) to yolo-jail,
including Apple's native container CLI as a first-class runtime alongside
Docker and Podman.

## Platform Support

- cli.py: IS_LINUX/IS_MACOS constants gate 20+ platform-specific branches
- entrypoint.py: dual-mode port forwarding (Unix sockets or TCP gateway)
- flake.nix: cross-build Linux images from macOS via imagePkgs/imageSystem
- All 489 unit tests pass, 1 skipped (cgroup lifecycle needs real cgroup v2)
- 27 integration tests pass on macOS with Docker/Colima
- End-to-end verified on Apple Container v0.11.0

## Three Container Runtimes on macOS

Auto-detection priority: container → podman → docker (native-first)

**Docker/Podman** (via Colima or Podman Machine):
- Shared Linux VM, virtiofs file sharing
- TCP gateway for port forwarding (virtiofs rejects Unix sockets)
- Docker named volume for mise (host binaries are Mach-O, not ELF)
- Skip -u UID:GID (VM handles ownership via virtiofs)
- Cgroups read-only inside container

**Apple Container** (native Virtualization.framework):
- Per-container Linux VM with own kernel
- Native --publish-socket for Unix socket forwarding
- Native --cpus/--memory for per-container resource limits
- Auto-defaults to half host CPUs and RAM (min 2 CPU, 4GB)
- Writable cgroups v2 — yolo-cglimit works for per-job limits
- Podman-in-Podman works (/dev/fuse + fuse-overlayfs)
- OCI image conversion via skopeo (no Docker dependency)
- Consolidated ws_state mount to stay under ~22 bind mount limit
- Plain --tmpfs paths (no options syntax)

## macOS Doctor Checks

- Nix daemon connectivity with timeout detection
- Linux builder configuration (remote builder for cross-building)
- Container VM backend status (Colima/Podman Machine)
- Nix store APFS volume mount
- Apple Container system status
- skopeo availability for OCI conversion
- Cgroup limitation notice

## macOS-Specific Fixes

- virtiofs rejects Unix sockets → TCP gateway via host.docker.internal
- /tmp → /private/tmp symlink → Path.resolve() for bind mounts
- Colima tmpfs defaults to mode 755 → explicit --tmpfs /tmp:exec,mode=1777
- Bootstrap stdout leak → redirect to stderr
- Container always gets YOLO_RUNTIME=podman (podman built into image)
- Apple Container ~22 bind mount limit → consolidated /home/agent mount
- Apple Container OCI-only images → skopeo/podman/docker conversion chain

## CI

- check-macos: unit tests on macos-latest (M1 ARM64)
- integration-macos: Docker integration on macos-15-intel (nested virt)
- test_macos_paths.py: 28 tests exercising all IS_MACOS and Apple Container
  code paths with mocked Docker/Nix (run on any platform)

## Documentation

- docs/macos.md: full setup guide (Podman, Docker/Colima, Apple Container)
- docs/platform-comparison.md: 4-column feature matrix, architecture diagrams,
  runtime comparison table, cgroup/nesting analysis
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md: macOS Platform Notes, Apple Container quick start
- README.md: cross-platform feature, runtime config example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stale container handling and add startup debug banner

- Add find_existing_container() to detect stopped containers (not just running)
- Add _remove_stale_container() to clean up containers from unclean shutdowns
- Remove stale containers under workspace lock before creating new ones
- This also fixes --new when a same-name stopped container exists
- Add _print_startup_banner() showing version, host platform, runtime, and
  container name on stderr for debugging/log sharing
- Print banner in both attach-to-existing and create-new-container paths
- Extract _get_yolo_version() helper to deduplicate version resolution
- Add 15 new tests covering all new functions

* Fix CI: venv symlink test and macOS Nix cross-build

- test_venv_symlinks_resolve: create symlinks targeting in-container /mise
  path instead of host mise path, since host paths don't exist in the jail
- integration-macos: build jail image inside a Linux Docker container
  (via Colima) to avoid needing a Nix Linux builder on the macOS host
- Remove unused host Nix install step from macOS integration job
- Pin nixos/nix Docker image to 2.34.4 for reproducibility
- Bump Colima disk from 20GB to 30GB for Nix store headroom

* Fix Apple Container stale detection and add integration tests

- find_existing_container: use 'container ls --all' for Apple Container
  (default 'ls' only shows running containers, same as Docker 'ps')
- _remove_stale_container: use --force flag for Apple Container
- Add integration tests for stale container recovery (all backends):
  - test_stale_container_auto_removed: creates a stopped container, then
    verifies yolo auto-removes it and starts successfully
  - test_stale_container_new_flag: same with --new flag
  - test_startup_banner_present: verifies banner shows version, platform,
    runtime, and container name

* fix: Apple Container CLI check uses non-existent 'system info' subcommand

The check was calling 'container system info' which doesn't exist in the
Apple Container CLI. Changed to 'container system status' which is the
correct subcommand, and simplified the logic to a single call that checks
both availability and running state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove cgroup warning on macOS — not actionable by user

cgroups are a Linux kernel feature; warning about their absence on macOS
just adds noise to an otherwise clean check output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prefer Apple Container on macOS and fix stale removal message

- Change runtime auto-detection order on macOS to prefer native Apple
  Container over podman/docker (container → podman → docker)
- Fix stale container removal message to print to stderr (where the
  test and startup banner expect it) instead of rich console (stdout)
- Fix tests to mock _is_apple_container() which runs the actual binary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Apple Container detection fails on 'container CLI version' output

_is_apple_container() checked for 'Apple' in the version string, but
the actual output is 'container CLI version 0.11.0' with no 'Apple'.
Added 'container CLI version' as an alternate match. This caused the
runtime to skip container and fall through to podman (which was broken).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: runtime auto-detection verifies daemon connectivity

Previously, _runtime() and _runtime_for_check() only checked if the CLI
binary existed on PATH. This meant a broken podman (installed but machine
not running) would be selected over a working docker or container runtime.

Now both functions call _runtime_is_connectable() which runs 'rt info'
(or 'container system status' for Apple) to verify the daemon is actually
reachable before selecting a runtime. yolo check also shows connectivity
status — broken runtimes get a warning instead of a green checkmark.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Apple Container find_running_container used unsupported --filter flag

'container ls --filter' doesn't exist in Apple's CLI — the error was
silently swallowed, causing find_running_container to always return None.
This made yolo remove and recreate the container on every invocation
instead of exec'ing into the running one.

Fixed by using plain 'container ls' (which lists running containers by
default) and scanning the output, matching find_existing_container's
approach. Also tightened the unit test to assert --filter is not used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: enable Apple Container integration tests

All integration tests now run with the 'container' runtime when Apple
Container is available. Previously disabled pending end-to-end
verification — all 10 tests pass: basic command, blocked tools, file
ownership, workspace mount, stale container recovery, --new flag, and
startup banner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix formatting in cli.py and test files

Run ruff format to fix formatting issues caught by CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

encrypted backups, allows account filtering, and provides user-friendly
warnings and error messages. This improves accessibility for users who
prefer managing OTP accounts outside mobile apps.

Add:
- Standalone Dart CLI that loads .flauth backup via environment variable
- Filtering support for issuer and name via command line argument
- Output formatting optimized for shell usage
- Test coverage for core reader, filter, and output
- English and Chinese documentation for the new tool
- Makefile rule for building the executable

This change enables headless and automation-friendly workflows with
Flauth account data.

Add authenticated RSVP read/upsert endpoints for seeded events (#44)

This PR implements Phase 4 RSVP API support by enabling authenticated
users to read and upsert their RSVP state on existing seeded events. It
adds the missing social RSVP surface area while preserving current event
discovery behavior.

- **API surface (authenticated social endpoints)**
- Added `GET /api/v1/social/rsvps/{eventId}` to fetch the caller’s RSVP
for a specific event.
- Added `PUT /api/v1/social/rsvps/{eventId}` to create-or-update the
caller’s RSVP (`yes|no|maybe`).

- **RSVP domain implementation**
- Introduced `Rsvp` Eloquent model (UUID primary key, `responded_at`
cast).
- Added request validation via `UpsertRsvpRequest` (`response` enum
validation).
- Added `RsvpResource` for stable JSON shape (`userId`, `eventId`,
`response`, `respondedAt`).

- **Controller behavior**
- Added `RsvpController` with event existence checks and user-scoped
lookup/upsert logic.
  - Upsert now returns status by outcome:
    - `201` when RSVP is created
    - `200` when RSVP is updated
  - Returns `404` for missing event / missing RSVP on read.

- **Routing and contract docs**
- Wired RSVP endpoints into existing `auth:sanctum` social route group.
- Added `Social` OpenAPI tag and endpoint annotations for RSVP
read/upsert, including distinct `200`/`201` responses.

- **Feature coverage**
  - Added focused feature tests for:
    - auth required on RSVP read/write
    - create then update semantics on upsert
    - user-scoped RSVP retrieval
    - invalid response validation

```php
Route::middleware('auth:sanctum')->prefix('social')->group(function () {
    Route::get('/rsvps/{eventId}', [RsvpController::class, 'show']);
    Route::put('/rsvps/{eventId}', [RsvpController::class, 'upsert']);
});
```

commit.message:
docs: add `instructions/oversized` to `aipm.toml` JSON schema (#454)

* docs: add instructions/oversized to aipm.toml JSON schema

The instructions/oversized rule was added to the lint system with
configurable options (lines, characters, resolve-imports) but was
never registered in the JSON Schema for aipm.toml. This caused editors
and schema validators to reject valid configurations such as:

  [workspace.lints."instructions/oversized"]
  lines = 200
  characters = 20000
  resolve-imports = true

Add a dedicated patternProperties entry for instructions/oversized
with full documentation for all supported properties:
- level    — severity override (optional)
- lines    — maximum line count (default: 100)
- characters — maximum character count (default: 15000)
- resolve-imports — follow @import chains (default: false)
- ignore   — per-rule path exclusions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json

commit.message:
feat: implement tap & hold to select shelf items (#415)

## Summary

Implements tap & hold gesture to enter select mode on shelf items,
eliminating the need to scroll to the top to press the "Select" button.

**Key Features:**
- Long-press (500ms) on book card body enters select mode and
auto-selects the item
- Separate touch targets: drag handle (200ms) vs card body (500ms) to
avoid conflicts
- 10px movement tolerance to prevent activation during scrolling
- Mobile-focused UX improvement

**Resolves:** #414

---

## Changes

### New Files
- **`hooks/useLongPress.ts`** - Reusable long-press detection hook
  - Configurable delay (default 500ms)
  - Touch and mouse event support
  - Movement tolerance to distinguish from scrolling

### Modified Files
- **`hooks/useBookListView.ts`** - Added
`enterSelectModeWithSelection(bookId)` function
- **`components/Books/BookListItem.tsx`** - Integrated long-press
handlers on card body
- **`components/Books/DraggableBookList.tsx`** - Passes long-press
handler to items
- **`app/shelves/[id]/page.tsx`** - Wires up new functionality on mobile
list views

---

## Technical Details

### Touch Target Separation
- **Drag Handle (left side):** Long-press 200ms → Triggers drag-and-drop
- **Book Card Body:** Long-press 500ms → Enters select mode + selects
item

This eliminates timing conflicts between drag and select gestures.

### Behavior
1. User long-presses on book card (NOT in select mode)
2. After 500ms: enters select mode + selects that book
3. BulkActionBar appears at bottom
4. User can tap other books to select/deselect
5. Existing "Select" button still works

### Safeguards
- Long-press handlers only active when NOT already in select mode
- 10px movement tolerance cancels long-press (allows smooth scrolling)
- Drag handles unchanged (keep existing 200ms drag behavior)
- Desktop tables unchanged (not needed for desktop UX)

---

## Testing

✅ **All 4013 tests pass**

### Manual Testing Checklist
- [ ] Long-press on book card body enters select mode + selects item
- [ ] Long-press on drag handle still triggers drag-and-drop
- [ ] Quick tap on book card still navigates to detail page
- [ ] Scrolling doesn't accidentally trigger selection (10px tolerance)
- [ ] In select mode, long-press is disabled (tap to select/deselect)
- [ ] BulkActionBar appears/works correctly
- [ ] Desktop tables unaffected

---

## Mobile UX Benefits

**Before:** User must scroll to top → press "Select" button → scroll
back down → select items

**After:** User long-presses on any item → immediately in select mode
with that item selected → continue selecting

Much faster and more intuitive, especially on mobile devices.

---------

commit.message:
feat(kyverno): add 5 core DevSecOps ClusterPolicies

- disallow-privileged-containers (Enforce) - blocks privileged: true
- disallow-privilege-escalation (Enforce) - requires allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
- require-resource-limits (Audit) - CPU/memory limits and requests required
- disallow-latest-image-tag (Enforce) - blocks :latest and untagged images
- require-non-root-user (Audit) - containers must not run as root

Critical security policies use Enforce; behavioural policies use Audit
to allow assessment before hard enforcement. System namespaces (kube-system,
crossplane-system, argocd, kyverno, monitoring) are excluded.

Policies applied via kubectl_manifest after helm_release.kyverno in platform.tf.

commit.message:
fix: resolve stylix HM double-import build error

- Fix attribute name: homeModules.default → homeManagerModules.default
  nixpalette-hyprland only exposes homeManagerModules, not homeModules
- Set stylix.homeManagerIntegration.autoImport = false
  Stylix's NixOS module auto-injects homeModules.stylix into
  home-manager.sharedModules by default. Our explicit sharedModules
  addition (nixpalette-hyprland → nixpalette → stylix HM module) would
  load the same stylix lambda a second time, causing the read-only
  stylix.base16 option to be set twice.
  Disabling autoImport ensures the stylix HM module is loaded exactly
  once, via nixpalette-hyprland.homeManagerModules.default.
  nixpalette's hm.nix independently sets all required stylix options
  from the resolved theme, so followSystem is not needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add ADR 0004 for multi-agent PR review system

Document the architectural decision for the Hybrid B+C approach:
- Context: need for automated, multi-perspective PR review in AI-driven OSS
- 5 alternatives considered (Copilot, Orchestrator-only, Comment-only, MCP, external platforms)
- Decision: Hybrid B+C (GitHub Actions trigger + local Orchestrator + 3 reviewers + Evaluator)
- Key sub-decisions: gh CLI over MCP, client-side AI, 3-iteration loop limit, dual merge gate
- Consequences: vendor-agnostic, zero secrets, self-documenting audit trail
